title: Rapid Employee Onboarding Kit
description: 7/30-day onboarding for small teams (2-15 people) with no HR department. Vending and shop ops forward. Checklists, scripts, tracking.

roles.label: Route driver (vending)
roles.typical_week_1: Shadow full route, learn keys and cash rules, practice machine checks, solo run with buddy on radio day 5

roles.label: Warehouse / inventory
roles.typical_week_1: Par levels, pick paths, vendor labels, restock load-out with experienced driver

roles.label: Counter / customer-facing
roles.typical_week_1: POS, complaint handoff (see mf-tpl-001), rush-hour shadow, recovery script read-through

roles.label: Route filler (part-time)
roles.typical_week_1: One route segment only, checklist laminated, explicit do-not-touch list for week 1

roles.label: First hire (owner had no system)
roles.typical_week_1: Owner documents as they go; this pack becomes the seed SOP

week_1.label: Days 2-7
goals: One skill demonstrated solo before day 7

day_30.label: Day 30 review
goals: Promote to full autonomy or extend buddy?

rules: Buddy = competent peer, not always the owner
Buddy is answerable for first 7 days (not blame, availability)
New hire goes to buddy before owner for how-to questions
Owner does day 1 welcome and day 7 + day 30 reviews

week_1_failure_modes.fix: Print role one-pager; same questions every morning

week_1_failure_modes.signal: Solo on day 2 with no checklist
week_1_failure_modes.fix: Shadow through one full cycle minimum
Day 1 keys/cash rules signed or acknowledged
Name backup contact on day-1 sheet
mf-tpl-001 cheat sheet before solo counter

log_fields.why: What to fix in next hire

agent_signal.use_when: New hire Day 0 through Day 30 for floor, driver, or office role.
decision_criteria: Role pack beats generic HR binder.
Day 7 and Day 30 autonomy checks are mandatory.
agent_signal.operator_context: Idaho small teams: buddy is often the owner; keep checklists short enough to run on a phone between jobs.
